# Pathways Foot & Ankle - LLMs.txt
# Last updated: 2026-07-10
# Purpose: Provide AI engines with structured information about the practice

---

## PRACTICE OVERVIEW

Name: Pathways Foot & Ankle
Specialty: Podiatry (Foot and Ankle Medicine)
Location: Oregon City, Oregon
Address: 216 14th St, Oregon City, OR 97045
Phone: (971) 359-5299
Website: https://pathwaysfoot.com
Email: (Contact via website)

Founder: Dr. Craig E. Clifford, DPM, MHA
Credentials: Board Certified, American Board of Foot and Ankle Surgery; Board Certified, American Board of Podiatric Medicine

Practice Model: Direct Care (cash-based, no insurance billing)
Key Differentiators: Same-day appointments, no referrals needed, house calls available, no surprise billing

---

## CORE SERVICES

### Diagnostic Ultrasound
Painless, radiation-free imaging of tendons, ligaments, and nerves. Used to diagnose plantar fasciitis, tendon tears, neuromas, and cysts.

### Functional Medicine
Holistic approach treating root causes of foot pain including inflammation, metabolic issues, and nutritional deficiencies.

### Custom Orthotics
Prescription devices designed specifically for each patient's foot shape and biomechanics. Correct pressure points and prevent reinjury.

### Shockwave Therapy
Non-invasive sound wave therapy for plantar fasciitis, neuropathy, neuromas, and Achilles tendinitis. No needles, no downtime.

### Regenerative Injection Therapy
Options include prolotherapy, Regenerative Protein Array (RPA), and homeopathic injectables (Traumeel®, Zeel®, Injeel®). Treats chronic tendinitis, arthritis, and nerve pain.

### Cortisone Injections
Anti-inflammatory treatment for arthritis, tendinitis, and plantar fasciitis. Relief begins within days and lasts weeks to months.

### Minimally Invasive Surgery
Small incisions (<1 cm) for bunions, hammertoes, and neuromas. Less pain, faster recovery, no large scars.

### Routine Nail and Callus Care
Medical assessment and treatment of thick, fungal, or deformed nails and painful calluses. Ideal for diabetics and seniors.

---

## COMMON CONDITIONS TREATED

### Acute & Chronic Pain
- Achilles Tendinitis
- Ankle Arthritis
- Ankle Instability
- Foot and Toe Arthritis
- Fractures
- Heel Pain
- Neuromas & Nerve Pain
- Nonunions
- Plantar Fasciitis
- Posterior Tibial Tendinitis
- Sports Injuries
- Stress Fractures
- Tarsal Tunnel Syndrome
- Tendinitis

### Deformities & Skin Conditions
- Bunions
- Cavus Foot
- Corns/Calluses
- Flat Feet
- Hammertoes & Crossover Toes
- Ingrown Toenails
- Skin Cancer of the Foot
- Toenail Fungus
- Ulcers / Wounds
- Warts

### Systemic Conditions Affecting the Feet
- Auto-immune Diseases
- Connective Tissue Disorders
- Diabetes & Diabetic Foot
- Gout
- Parkinson's Disease
- Peripheral Neuropathy
- Peripheral Vascular Disease
- Raynaud's Disease

---

## FREQUENTLY ASKED QUESTIONS

**Q: Do I need a referral to see Dr. Clifford?**
A: No. Pathways Foot & Ankle does not require referrals. Patients can schedule directly for same-day or next-day appointments.

**Q: Does Pathways Foot & Ankle accept insurance?**
A: No. The practice operates on a direct care (cash-based) model. This allows for longer appointments, transparent pricing, and no insurance middlemen.

**Q: What is the cost of an appointment?**
A: Pricing is transparent and discussed upfront. Patients never receive surprise bills. HSA/FSA cards are accepted.

**Q: Does Dr. Clifford treat heel pain?**
A: Yes. Heel pain is one of the most common conditions treated. Options include shockwave therapy, custom orthotics, regenerative injections, and functional medicine.

**Q: Does Pathways Foot & Ankle treat toenail fungus?**
A: Yes. Treatment options include laser therapy, toenail removal, and routine nail care.

**Q: Are house calls available?**
A: Yes. House calls are available throughout the region for patients who cannot travel to the office.

**Q: What is Dr. Clifford's approach to surgery?**
A: Surgery is always a last resort. Dr. Clifford focuses on conservative, non-surgical treatment first and only recommends surgery when truly necessary.

---

## ABOUT DR. CLIFFORD

Dr. Craig E. Clifford is a board-certified foot and ankle specialist with over a decade of experience. He completed his residency at the Franciscan Foot and Ankle Institute in Washington, where he later served as Director of Residency Training.

Honors and recognition include:
- Physician of the Year (2020) - Washington State Podiatric Medical Association
- Clinician of the Year (2014, 2015, 2017, 2018, 2021, 2022)
- Instructor of the Year (2019)
- Outstanding Teaching Award (2015, 2020)

Dr. Clifford served on the Board of Directors for the International Foot and Ankle Foundation (2016-2026) and has volunteered with the Yucatán Children's Project in Mexico for over 10 years.
